channel.title: Light Tower Group — NYC CRE Capital Markets Insights
channel.description: Data-driven capital markets analysis and building intelligence for NYC commercial real estate professionals.

item.title: Infinity and KB Realty's $12M SoHo Retail Buy Shows Basis Still Beats Story
item.description: Infinity Real Estate and KB Realty Partners buy 75 Kenmare Street retail condo for $11.75M. The deal reveals how basis, tenant mix, and acquisition financing still work in Manhattan retail.

news:news.news:title: Infinity and KB Realty's $12M SoHo Retail Buy Shows Basis Still Beats Story
news:news.news:keywords: Infinity Real Estate, KB Realty Partners, 75 Kenmare Street, SoHo retail, retail condo

item.title: Madison Realty's $211M Asbury Park Loan Tests Condo Construction Debt's Return
item.description: Madison Realty Capital and Cottonwood Group provide $211M construction loan for LIDO Asbury Park, a 112-unit luxury condo. The deal tests private credit's willingness to finance for-sale development.

news:news.news:title: Madison Realty's $211M Asbury Park Loan Tests Condo Construction Debt's Return
news:news.news:keywords: construction financing, Madison Realty Capital, Asbury Park, luxury condominium, private credit

item.title: JLL Hires Retail Veterans: Betting on Experience, Not Square Footage
item.description: JLL hires Dreamscape co-founders Strauss and Zinovoy to lead experiential retail leasing. The move reveals how brokerage talent is following capital toward placemaking over pure space.

news:news.news:title: JLL Hires Retail Veterans: Betting on Experience, Not Square Footage
news:news.news:keywords: JLL, experiential retail, retail leasing, talent moves, mixed-use

item.title: Rialto-Hines $229M Refi Shows Office Debt Is Available Only for the Right Basis and Sponsor
item.description: Rialto Capital and Hines provide $228.9M floating-rate bridge loan for 295 Fifth Avenue. The deal reveals how office debt is being underwritten today: sponsor credibility, renovated assets, and a clear path to stabilization.

news:news.news:title: Rialto-Hines $229M Refi Shows Office Debt Is Available Only for the Right Basis and Sponsor
news:news.news:keywords: Rialto Capital, Hines, 295 Fifth Avenue, office refinancing, bridge debt, Midtown South, PGIM, Tribeca Investment Group, Meadow Partners, Walker & Dunlop

item.title: M&T's $141M Refi Shows Time Is the Cost of Capital in Manhattan Multifamily
item.description: M&T Realty Capital provides $141M bridge loan for Anagram Turtle Bay. The deal shows how fast lease-up compresses refinancing risk and lender underwriting windows.

news:news.news:title: M&T's $141M Refi Shows Time Is the Cost of Capital in Manhattan Multifamily
news:news.news:keywords: M&T Realty Capital, Global Holdings, MAG Partners, Safanad, Anagram Turtle Bay, multifamily refinancing, Manhattan multifamily, bridge debt, lease-up risk, capital markets

item.title: Oil-Driven Yield Spike Tightens the Clock on CRE Refinancings
item.description: US 10- and 30-year yields hit two-month highs as crude oil surges. For CRE owners with maturing loans, the cost of carry just rose and the window to lock fixed-rate debt narrowed.

news:news.news:title: Oil-Driven Yield Spike Tightens the Clock on CRE Refinancings
news:news.news:keywords: Treasury yields, inflation risk, refinancing, floating-rate debt, Fed policy

item.title: Icahn's Pep Boys Sale: The Basis Is the Deal, Not the Brand
item.description: Icahn sells Pep Boys for $700M but keeps the real estate. The basis spread reveals a capital strategy that separates operating risk from property value.

news:news.news:title: Icahn's Pep Boys Sale: The Basis Is the Deal, Not the Brand
news:news.news:keywords: Icahn, Pep Boys, Mavis Tire, retail real estate, sale-leaseback

item.title: SROA's $750M Fund X Shows Self-Storage Roll-Up Still Works When Capital Is Patient
item.description: SROA Capital targets $750M for Fund X to acquire value-add self-storage assets. The strategy reveals that roll-up economics still attract institutional capital when the operator controls the operating platform.

news:news.news:title: SROA's $750M Fund X Shows Self-Storage Roll-Up Still Works When Capital Is Patient
news:news.news:keywords: self-storage, value-add, fundraising, SROA Capital, roll-up strategy

item.title: East Harlem RFP Tests Whether City Land Can Deliver Affordable Housing Without Subsidy
item.description: NYCEDC issues RFP for 140-unit B-East site in East Harlem requiring 485-x affordable housing without public subsidy. The bids will test developer appetite and project viability.

news:news.news:title: East Harlem RFP Tests Whether City Land Can Deliver Affordable Housing Without Subsidy
news:news.news:keywords: NYCEDC, affordable housing, 485-x, East Harlem, development RFP, city-owned land, mixed-use

item.title: Brennan's Boise Buy Shows Industrial Capital Is Chasing Lease-Up Risk, Not Speculation
item.description: Brennan Investment Group buys a 439,600 SF industrial portfolio in Nampa, ID. The deal signals that institutional capital is willing to pay for stabilized cash flow in secondary markets but not for development risk.

news:news.news:title: Brennan's Boise Buy Shows Industrial Capital Is Chasing Lease-Up Risk, Not Speculation
news:news.news:keywords: Brennan Investment Group, industrial real estate, Boise market, secondary markets, underwriting margin, lease-up risk, capital flows

item.title: Sale-Leaseback of Texas Industrial Building Shows Capital Is Chasing Cash Flow, Not Stories
item.description: Partners Real Estate brokers sale-leaseback of 55,700 SF industrial building in New Caney, Texas. Buyer undisclosed. The deal reveals capital is prioritizing cash flow over growth stories.

news:news.news:title: Sale-Leaseback of Texas Industrial Building Shows Capital Is Chasing Cash Flow, Not Stories
news:news.news:keywords: sale-leaseback, industrial, Houston, capital markets, yield

item.title: Rosewood's San Antonio Start Shows Development Is Alive Where Basis and Demand Align
item.description: Rosewood Property Co. begins leasing 359-unit Phase III in San Antonio's Alamo Heights. The project signals development capital is flowing to submarkets with strong demand and manageable basis.

news:news.news:title: Rosewood's San Antonio Start Shows Development Is Alive Where Basis and Demand Align
news:news.news:keywords: multifamily development, San Antonio, Rosewood Property Co., construction lending, capital markets

item.title: 72% Occupied Retail Trade: What a Credit Committee Would Ask Before Lending
item.description: Marcus & Millichap sells Commerce Centre in Prairieville, LA at 72% occupancy. For lenders, the risk is the income gap between current and stabilized cash flow.

news:news.news:title: 72% Occupied Retail Trade: What a Credit Committee Would Ask Before Lending
news:news.news:keywords: retail, occupancy, lending, credit, Prairieville, Louisiana

item.title: The Fleet's 450 Units Near Completion: A Test of Brooklyn's Rental Demand at Today's Rent Basis
item.description: The Fleet's 450-unit tower nears completion in Downtown Brooklyn. The Jay Group faces a lease-up test at current rents, revealing whether supply can absorb at today's pricing.

news:news.news:title: The Fleet's 450 Units Near Completion: A Test of Brooklyn's Rental Demand at Today's Rent Basis
news:news.news:keywords: Downtown Brooklyn, The Fleet, The Jay Group, rental demand, lease-up risk

item.title: Starhill Phase II Shows Affordable Housing Works When the Capital Stack Is Built for It
item.description: The $172M Starhill Phase II in Morris Heights shows how subsidy, debt, and operating discipline align to produce 244 affordable units. The capital stack is the story.

news:news.news:title: Starhill Phase II Shows Affordable Housing Works When the Capital Stack Is Built for It
news:news.news:keywords: affordable housing, capital stack, New York City, Bronx, subsidy, development finance, ELLA term sheet

item.title: WildFire Sale Shows Private Equity Can Exit Energy at Scale—If the Buyer Has a Lower Cost of Capital
item.description: Warburg Pincus and Kayne Anderson sell WildFire Energy to Magnolia Oil & Gas for $4.06B. The deal shows how private equity exits energy when public markets offer cheaper capital.

news:news.news:title: WildFire Sale Shows Private Equity Can Exit Energy at Scale—If the Buyer Has a Lower Cost of Capital
news:news.news:keywords: private equity, energy, M&A, capital markets, exit strategy

item.title: Berkadia's $35.5M Castro Valley Sale: Value-Add Multifamily Trades Only When the Underwriting Is Defensible
item.description: Berkadia arranges $35.5M sale of two value-add multifamily properties in Castro Valley, CA. Felson Cos. sells to Prime Residential. The deal reveals the underwriting conditions that separate investable deals from attractive stories.

news:news.news:title: Berkadia's $35.5M Castro Valley Sale: Value-Add Multifamily Trades Only When the Underwriting Is Defensible
news:news.news:keywords: Berkadia, multifamily, value-add, Castro Valley, Freddie Mac

item.title: Intercontinental's $69.5M Buy Shows Grocery-Anchored Retail Still Commands a Premium for Stability
item.description: Intercontinental Real Estate pays $69.5M for fully leased Lakeland Town Center in Auburn, WA. The deal reveals how capital is pricing stability over growth in retail.

news:news.news:title: Intercontinental's $69.5M Buy Shows Grocery-Anchored Retail Still Commands a Premium for Stability
news:news.news:keywords: Intercontinental Real Estate, Lakeland Town Center, grocery-anchored retail, Seattle retail, JLL Capital Markets

item.title: A $7M Loan in Pataskala Tests Which Industrial Assets Still Attract Bank Debt
item.description: Associated Bank provides $7M for a 75,250-SF industrial outdoor storage building in Pataskala, OH, leased to Thayer Power & Communication. The deal reveals how regional banks are selectively financing niche industrial assets with conservative leverage.

news:news.news:title: A $7M Loan in Pataskala Tests Which Industrial Assets Still Attract Bank Debt
news:news.news:keywords: industrial outdoor storage, regional bank lending, Ohio industrial, Transport Properties, GFH Partners

item.title: A $15M Sale in Wausau Shows Multifamily Liquidity Is Narrow but Real
item.description: Marcus & Millichap brokers $15M sale of 75-unit multifamily in Wausau, WI. The deal reveals how new construction, local capital, and a clear basis can still clear in a thin transaction market.

news:news.news:title: A $15M Sale in Wausau Shows Multifamily Liquidity Is Narrow but Real
news:news.news:keywords: multifamily, Wausau, Marcus & Millichap, secondary market, liquidity

item.title: Vacant Lower Manhattan Building Sale Shows Redevelopment Is a Capital Game, Not a Market Signal
item.description: Matthews negotiates $15.2M sale of vacant 19,873-SF mixed-use building at 158 Lafayette St., Lower Manhattan. The deal reveals redevelopment capital discipline, not market confidence.

news:news.news:title: Vacant Lower Manhattan Building Sale Shows Redevelopment Is a Capital Game, Not a Market Signal
news:news.news:keywords: Matthews, Lower Manhattan, mixed-use, redevelopment, vacant building, 158 Lafayette St, capital markets, New York City

item.title: GO Residential REIT’s $109M Crown Heights Buy Shows Basis Is the Only Signal That Matters
item.description: GO Residential REIT paid $109M for an 81% stake in a Crown Heights multifamily building. The basis, tax exemption, and retained seller stake reveal how capital is pricing Brooklyn apartments in mid-2026.

news:news.news:title: GO Residential REIT’s $109M Crown Heights Buy Shows Basis Is the Only Signal That Matters
news:news.news:keywords: GO Residential REIT, Crown Heights, multifamily, Brooklyn, basis, 421-a, FBE Limited, Adam America, Zev Marmurstein

item.title: Manhattan Luxury New Dev Contracts Drop as Pipeline Shrinks, Not Demand
item.description: Manhattan luxury new development contracts fell to 12 in four weeks, down from a 28-deal average. The 62% inventory drop reveals a supply squeeze, but underwriting margins are thinning as buyers demand discounts.

news:news.news:title: Manhattan Luxury New Dev Contracts Drop as Pipeline Shrinks, Not Demand
news:news.news:keywords: Manhattan, luxury, new development, pipeline, Olshan Realty, Jonathan Miller, supply constraint, pricing, underwriting

item.title: Zack Wheeler's $26M Soho Flip Shows Luxury Condo Liquidity Is Narrow but Real
item.description: Zack Wheeler sold his Soho penthouse for $26M, $5,400 per square foot. The flip shows luxury condo liquidity is narrow but real when basis, timing, and product align.

news:news.news:title: Zack Wheeler's $26M Soho Flip Shows Luxury Condo Liquidity Is Narrow but Real
news:news.news:keywords: luxury condos, Soho, New York City, flip, residential market

item.title: JLL's $24M Student Housing Refi Shows Floating-Rate Debt Still Has a Bid for the Right Basis
item.description: JLL arranges $24M floating-rate refi for The Row student housing near University of Florida. The deal shows lenders will finance stabilized assets with strong sponsorship and a defensible basis.

news:news.news:title: JLL's $24M Student Housing Refi Shows Floating-Rate Debt Still Has a Bid for the Right Basis
news:news.news:keywords: student housing, refinancing, floating-rate debt, JLL Capital Markets, GID, Ardent Cos., University of Florida, Gainesville

item.title: Grubb's $1.9B REIT Rollup Shows Time Is the Cost of Capital in Multifamily
item.description: Grubb Properties merges funds into a $1.9B nontraded REIT, securing $617M in financing. The move reveals how time, not just rate, shapes multifamily capital strategy.

news:news.news:title: Grubb's $1.9B REIT Rollup Shows Time Is the Cost of Capital in Multifamily
news:news.news:keywords: Grubb Properties, nontraded REIT, multifamily, capital markets, recapitalization

item.title: Prologis's Third Bid for Segro Shows Time Is the Costliest Capital
item.description: Prologis's $18.2B third bid for Segro, rejected but not dead, reveals how time pressure and rising costs are reshaping industrial REIT M&A.

news:news.news:title: Prologis's Third Bid for Segro Shows Time Is the Costliest Capital
news:news.news:keywords: Prologis, Segro, industrial REIT, M&A, capital markets

item.title: Cooler Master Lease Shows Industrial Demand Is Narrowing to Build-to-Suit
item.description: Cooler Master's 97,285 SF lease at EastGroup's Grand West Crossing shows industrial demand concentrating on new, build-to-suit space. Landlords absorb vacancy risk.

news:news.news:title: Cooler Master Lease Shows Industrial Demand Is Narrowing to Build-to-Suit
news:news.news:keywords: industrial leasing, Houston, Cooler Master, EastGroup Properties, build-to-suit, JLL, Grand West Crossing

item.title: Brookfield's $1B Medical Office Bet: Capital for Control, Not Just Yield
item.description: Brookfield pays $1B for a 49% stake in Healthpeak's $2.1B medical office portfolio. The deal reveals how institutional capital is trading yield for control in a bifurcated market.

news:news.news:title: Brookfield's $1B Medical Office Bet: Capital for Control, Not Just Yield
news:news.news:keywords: Brookfield, Healthpeak, medical office, joint venture, capital markets

item.title: Grubb's $617M Capital Stack Shows Multifamily Scale Still Commands Liquidity
item.description: JLL secures $617M for Grubb Properties across two REITs and a Manhattan development. The deal structure reveals how capital is flowing to scale, not to stories.

news:news.news:title: Grubb's $617M Capital Stack Shows Multifamily Scale Still Commands Liquidity
news:news.news:keywords: Grubb Properties, JLL Capital Markets, multifamily, REIT, capital stack, New York development

item.title: Webster Bank's Two-Year Term on Bronx Refi Is a Risk Signal, Not a Confidence Vote
item.description: Webster Bank provides $26.3M two-year fixed-rate loan for The Bridgeline in the Bronx. The short term signals banks are lending but managing duration risk, not betting on the cycle.

news:news.news:title: Webster Bank's Two-Year Term on Bronx Refi Is a Risk Signal, Not a Confidence Vote
news:news.news:keywords: Webster Bank, JCAL Development, Cushman & Wakefield, The Bridgeline, Bronx multifamily, refinancing, bank lending, fixed-rate loan, two-year loan, regional banks

item.title: Brookfield's $1B Healthpeak Stake: REITs Are Selling Control to Buy Time
item.description: Brookfield pays $1B for 49% of Healthpeak's $2.1B medical office portfolio. The deal reveals REITs' need for non-dilutive capital and institutional appetite for yield at scale.

news:news.news:title: Brookfield's $1B Healthpeak Stake: REITs Are Selling Control to Buy Time
news:news.news:keywords: Brookfield, Healthpeak, medical office, joint venture, REIT capital

item.title: AI Data Centers' 125 GW Gap Forces Utilities and Developers Into a New Capital Calculus
item.description: BofA projects 125 GW of data center load by 2030, but utilities plan only 93 GW of new capacity. The gap reshapes capital allocation for onsite generation, gas engines, and battery storage.

news:news.news:title: AI Data Centers' 125 GW Gap Forces Utilities and Developers Into a New Capital Calculus
news:news.news:keywords: AI data centers, electricity supply gap, onsite generation, natural gas engines, battery storage, utility capacity planning, Bank of America

item.title: The $229M Textile Building Refinancing: Floating-Rate Bridge Debt on a Stabilized Asset Is a Bet on Timing, Not Value
item.description: Walker & Dunlop arranges $228.9M floating-rate bridge loan for 295 Fifth Avenue. The deal reveals how office refinancing requires expensive, short-dated capital even for stabilized assets.

news:news.news:title: The $229M Textile Building Refinancing: Floating-Rate Bridge Debt on a Stabilized Asset Is a Bet on Timing, Not Value
news:news.news:keywords: Walker & Dunlop, 295 Fifth Avenue, Textile Building, office refinancing, floating-rate debt

item.title: Harrison Street's Student Housing Refi Shows Private Credit Is Picking the Right Basis
item.description: Harrison Street provides a loan to refinance VERVE Boise, a 547-bed student housing community near Boise State. The deal reveals how private credit is underwriting stabilized, recently delivered assets with strong lease-up.

news:news.news:title: Harrison Street's Student Housing Refi Shows Private Credit Is Picking the Right Basis
news:news.news:keywords: Harrison Street, student housing, refinancing, private credit, Boise State University, Appian Capital, Subtext

item.title: Brookfield and CPP's $5.2B Industrial Take-Private: A Bet on Basis, Not Just Demand
item.description: Brookfield and CPPIB's $5.2B all-cash bid for LXP Industrial Trust shows institutional capital is buying control, not just exposure. The 12.3% premium and 108-property portfolio reveal a bet on basis, balance sheet, and long-term demand.

news:news.news:title: Brookfield and CPP's $5.2B Industrial Take-Private: A Bet on Basis, Not Just Demand
news:news.news:keywords: Brookfield, CPPIB, LXP Industrial Trust, industrial real estate, take-private, institutional capital, capital markets

item.title: A 45-Room Hotel Sale in Detroit Lakes Reveals Where Capital Is Willing to Go
item.description: Marcus & Millichap sells a 45-room Country Inn & Suites in Detroit Lakes, MN. The deal signals that capital is flowing to secondary markets with defensible cash flow and recent renovation.

news:news.news:title: A 45-Room Hotel Sale in Detroit Lakes Reveals Where Capital Is Willing to Go
news:news.news:keywords: Marcus & Millichap, hotel sale, Detroit Lakes, secondary market, hospitality capital

item.title: Texas Industrial Sale Shows Capital Is Concentrating, Not Flowing
item.description: Partners Real Estate brokers sale of 43,000-SF industrial building in La Marque, Texas. The deal shows capital is concentrating in small, single-tenant assets with clear demand.

news:news.news:title: Texas Industrial Sale Shows Capital Is Concentrating, Not Flowing
news:news.news:keywords: industrial, Texas, La Marque, Partners Real Estate, single-tenant

item.title: PEF Advisors' Quail Run Buy Shows Affordable Housing Capital Is Picking Basis, Not Stories
item.description: PEF Advisors acquires 104-unit Quail Run Apartments in San Leandro, Calif. The deal shows preservation capital is underwriting proven LIHTC assets with existing rent restrictions, not future subsidy promises.

news:news.news:title: PEF Advisors' Quail Run Buy Shows Affordable Housing Capital Is Picking Basis, Not Stories
news:news.news:keywords: affordable housing, LIHTC, preservation, PEF Advisors, San Leandro, multifamily, capital markets

item.title: A $5.2M Industrial Sale in Elk Grove Village Shows the Market Is Pricing Function, Not Location
item.description: Sonica International pays $5.2M for a 39,879-sf industrial building in Elk Grove Village, IL. The deal reveals how user demand and basis discipline are driving small-bay industrial liquidity.

news:news.news:title: A $5.2M Industrial Sale in Elk Grove Village Shows the Market Is Pricing Function, Not Location
news:news.news:keywords: industrial, Chicago, Elk Grove Village, user sale, Sonica International, Lee & Associates

item.title: Northlake Mall Sale Shows Healthcare Is the Only Anchor That Can Reprice a Dead Mall
item.description: Centurion Foundation buys Northlake Mall for $95M, plans Emory Healthcare expansion. The deal shows how medical-anchored repurposing resets basis and unlocks capital.

news:news.news:title: Northlake Mall Sale Shows Healthcare Is the Only Anchor That Can Reprice a Dead Mall
news:news.news:keywords: Northlake Mall, Centurion Foundation, Emory Healthcare, mall repurposing, Atlanta CRE

item.title: Axonic's $72M Park Avenue Loan: The Underwriting Condition That Made It Work
item.description: Axonic Capital arranges $72M floating-rate loan for 430 Park Avenue leasehold. The 99% occupancy and sponsor structure reveal the narrow underwriting conditions for office debt in 2026.

news:news.news:title: Axonic's $72M Park Avenue Loan: The Underwriting Condition That Made It Work
news:news.news:keywords: office refinancing, leasehold interest, Axonic Capital, Midtown Manhattan, floating-rate debt, Ackman-Ziff, Oestreicher Properties, Midwood Investment, Marx Realty

item.title: JLL's $176.6M Student Housing Loan Tests Construction Debt's New Underwriting Margin
item.description: JLL secures $176.6M construction loan for 1,395-bed student housing near UCF. The deal reveals how lenders underwrite construction risk in 2026: narrow basis, strong sponsor, and demand visibility.

news:news.news:title: JLL's $176.6M Student Housing Loan Tests Construction Debt's New Underwriting Margin
news:news.news:keywords: student housing, construction financing, JLL Capital Markets, Beachwold Residential, University of Central Florida

item.title: Brixton Capital's $40.9M Loan Shows Life Companies Are Picking Multifamily Winners
item.description: JLL arranges $40.9M acquisition loan for Allura Las Colinas via a life insurance company. The deal shows life insurers are selectively financing multifamily at the right basis.

news:news.news:title: Brixton Capital's $40.9M Loan Shows Life Companies Are Picking Multifamily Winners
news:news.news:keywords: multifamily, life insurance company lending, acquisition financing, Brixton Capital, JLL, Irving, Texas

item.title: Self-Storage Refi Shows Debt Is Available for the Right Basis, Not the Right Sector
item.description: Affinius Capital and Axonic Capital provide $42.5M for UTEX Storage Partners' two-property Miami self-storage portfolio. The refi shows debt is flowing to supply-constrained submarkets with credible sponsors, not to sectors broadly.

news:news.news:title: Self-Storage Refi Shows Debt Is Available for the Right Basis, Not the Right Sector
news:news.news:keywords: self-storage, Miami, refinancing, Affinius Capital, Axonic Capital, UTEX Storage Partners, Greysteel

item.title: MG Properties' Portland Buy Shows Agency Debt Is Buying Time, Not Momentum
item.description: MG Properties buys 188-unit Portland apartment complex with Freddie Mac financing. The deal shows agency debt is buying time for sponsors, not momentum.

news:news.news:title: MG Properties' Portland Buy Shows Agency Debt Is Buying Time, Not Momentum
news:news.news:keywords: MG Properties, Freddie Mac, Portland, multifamily, agency debt

item.title: Whitehall Plaza Sale Shows Retail Capital Is Chasing Income, Not Stories
item.description: JLL sells Whitehall Plaza, a 365,071 SF shopping center in PA, to Mishorim Gold Group. The deal signals retail capital is flowing to assets with proven income streams and operator upgrades.

news:news.news:title: Whitehall Plaza Sale Shows Retail Capital Is Chasing Income, Not Stories
news:news.news:keywords: retail, shopping center, capital markets, JLL, Mishorim Gold Group, Whitehall Plaza, Lehigh Valley, asset management

item.title: Denver Apartment Sale at 55% Occupancy Shows Basis, Not Demand, Drives Trades
item.description: Marcus & Millichap sells 93-unit Denver apartment at 55% occupancy. The deal reveals how repriced basis, not demand, is unlocking liquidity for multifamily owners.

news:news.news:title: Denver Apartment Sale at 55% Occupancy Shows Basis, Not Demand, Drives Trades
news:news.news:keywords: Denver multifamily, basis reset, liquidity, Marcus & Millichap, occupancy distress

item.title: Hudson Landing's $1B Price Tag Tests Manhattan's Development Math
item.description: Gotham, Fisher Brothers, and MURAL plan 1,127 units at 621 West 45th Street. The $1B price tag and brownfield remediation reveal the capital required to build in Manhattan today.

news:news.news:title: Hudson Landing's $1B Price Tag Tests Manhattan's Development Math
news:news.news:keywords: Hudson Landing, Gotham Organization, Fisher Brothers, MURAL Real Estate Group, FXCollaborative, Hell's Kitchen, Manhattan development, brownfield remediation, affordable housing, Intrepid Museum

item.title: Jadian's $500M WareSpace Bet Shows Capital Is Chasing Operating Control, Not Just Real Estate
item.description: Jadian Capital upsizes WareSpace equity to $500M+, backing a micro-bay industrial platform. The move reveals capital's preference for operating control over passive asset ownership.

news:news.news:title: Jadian's $500M WareSpace Bet Shows Capital Is Chasing Operating Control, Not Just Real Estate
news:news.news:keywords: Jadian Capital, WareSpace, micro-bay industrial, alternative real estate, equity commitment, platform building, small business storage
